title: U Mass - Non-esterified fatty acids
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dngu3","text":"Summary: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b3o8v","text":"\nThis experiment measures serum and plasma concentrations of non-esterified fatty acids using a 96-well kit. The experiment involves a coupled reaction to measure non-esterified fatty acids (NEFA) which ultimately forms a purple product that absorbs light at 550nm. This allows the concentration of NEFA to be determined from the optical density measured at 540~550nm. Serum fatty acids levels reflect systemic lipid metabolism, lipid digestion/absorption, and lipid clearance. Serum fatty acids levels are altered in obesity, insulin resistance, and type 2 diabetes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start:
{"blocks":[{"key":"aqgan","text":"Notes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1s03d","text":"
√  Freshly prepared blood or plasma samples are recommended. If storing samples, keep blood and plasma samples at   –20ºC or at -70ºC for long-term storage. Avoid freeze/thaw cycles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cpknb","text":"
√  Avoid using samples with gross hemolysis or lipemia.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"biina","text":"
√  Allow all reagents to come to room temperature before measurement.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Add 5 μl (or 1~10 μl) of serum or plasma sample to a well of Plate A.
Add dilution buffer to each well to reach a total sample volume of 50 μl.
Addition of 5 μl results in a 10x dilution of sample (5 μl of serum/plasma sample in 50 μl total sample volume).
Add 50 μl of each standard to empty wells. Use Plate B if necessary.
Add 10.5 ml of the reconstituted FFA Reagent A to one of the disposable trays provided with the kit.
Add 100 μl of FFA Reagent A to each well.
Gently shake the plate to ensure thorough mixing.
Place in a 37ºC incubator for 10 minutes.
Add 5.5 ml of the reconstituted FFA Reagent B to the other disposable tray provided with the kit.
Add 50 μl of FFA Reagent B to each well.
Gently shake the plate to ensure thorough mixing.
Place in a 37ºC incubator for 10 minutes.
Allow the plate to equilibrate to room temperature for 5 minutes. During this time, ensure that there are no bubbles in the solution mixture.
Use a large gauge needle or clean pipet tip to pop any bubbles as this will result in inaccurate absorbance readings.
Measure the optical density of each well at 540 nm.
creator: Lili Liang
